prompt: An elderly tailor's workshop, dimly lit by a single hanging lamp. On a mannequin stands an extraordinarily fitted suit — every seam curved to follow an unusual body, patches sewn around an old scar, one shoulder padded to match a slight hunch. Beside it, a normal man tries to pull on an identical suit, but the arms are wrong, the waist impossibly narrow in one spot. Dust motes in warm amber light. Photorealistic, film photography style, 35mm, shallow depth of field. No text, no labels, no diagrams, no floating elements
metaphor: A suit tailored so precisely to one person it fits no one else — perfect memorization of one case, failing all others.
